name: Ohio

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the Ohio Division of Real Estate & Professional Licensing, the agency within the Ohio Department of Commerce overseen by the Ohio Real Estate Commission. The 80-question national portion and the 40-question Ohio portion are scored separately, needing 70 percent on each.

intro:
The Ohio real estate salesperson exam is a tougher test than its reputation suggests. The Ohio Division of Real Estate & Professional Licensing, the agency within the Department of Commerce that the Ohio Real Estate Commission oversees, scores its national and Ohio state-law portions separately, so a strong national score won't rescue a thin grasp of Ohio law. That split is exactly why Ohio-specific preparation carries as much weight as the national material.

Ohio tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches, from the five-member Ohio Real Estate Commission and the powers the Division uses to investigate and discipline licensees, to the agency disclosure statement and the Consumer Guide to Agency Relationships an agent must provide under Ohio Revised Code Chapter 4735, the dual agency and in-company transaction rules that govern how one brokerage handles both sides of a deal, the Residential Property Disclosure Form required by section 5302.30 on one to four family homes, and the Real Estate Recovery Fund that repays consumers harmed by licensee misconduct. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need Ohio-specific practice questions.

faq.q: How many questions are on the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: The Ohio Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 120 scored multiple-choice questions, split into an 80-question national portion and a 40-question Ohio state-law portion. PSI delivers both portions in one computer session and mixes in five to ten unscored experimental questions that look just like the scored ones, so the time spent on those does not count against your result.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: 70 percent on each portion, scored separately. That is 56 of the 80 national questions and 28 of the 40 Ohio questions. If you pass one portion and fail the other you only retake the portion you failed, as long as you finish both within the year on your eligibility label.

faq.q: How much does the Ohio real estate exam cost?
faq.a: PSI charges $63 to sit the salesperson exam, and that one fee covers both the national and Ohio portions taken in the same session. A retake of one or both portions runs the same kind of PSI fee. The fee is not refundable or transferable and is forfeited if you do not test within a year of paying it.

faq.q: How long is the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: Three hours total. PSI allows two hours for the 80-question national portion and one hour for the 40-question Ohio portion, taken in one sitting. PSI runs Ohio test centers in Akron, Cincinnati, Cleveland, Columbus, Toledo, and a handful of other cities, and also offers a remote online proctored option, with your unofficial score printed or shown before you leave.

faq.q: Who administers the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: The Ohio Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered by PSI for the Ohio Division of Real Estate & Professional Licensing, overseen by the Ohio Real Estate Commission. You register and schedule your exam date through PSI.

faq.q: Is the Ohio real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It is a serious test, and the part that catches people off guard is the 40-question Ohio portion. The national half covers the same principles taught in every state, but the Ohio questions reward candidates who actually studied the state's agency disclosure rules, the license law and Commission rules in Revised Code Chapter 4735, and the duties and powers of the Division of Real Estate. Most people who struggle leaned too heavily on national material and underprepared on Ohio law. Honest reps on Ohio-specific practice questions are the difference between a first-time pass and a retake.

faq.q: What's on the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: The 80-question national portion covers property ownership, land-use controls, valuation, financing, agency, property condition and disclosures, contracts, transfer of title, the practice of real estate, real estate math, and specialty topics, with the practice of real estate, contracts, and agency carrying the most weight on PSI's content outline. The 40-question Ohio portion covers the governance and powers of the Division and the Ohio Real Estate Commission, licensing rules, the license law and rules in Revised Code Chapter 4735 and Administrative Code 1301:5, and Ohio agency law, including the agency disclosure statement, dual agency, confidentiality, and the Canon of Ethics. License law and agency carry the heaviest weight on the state portion, so they are worth extra attention.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish Ohio's required pre-license coursework, the highest-leverage move is grinding Ohio-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National principles you can pick up from any course, but the 40-question Ohio portion rewards repetition on the agency disclosure statement, the dual agency and in-company transaction rules, the Real Estate Recovery Fund, and the duties and powers of the Ohio Real Estate Commission. The RealReady app gives you the full Ohio question bank plus the national bank, detailed explanations, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where you are weak.

The 20 Ohio practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the Ohio bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the Ohio real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated Ohio question bank built for the Ohio real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the Ohio law that trips people up, like the agency disclosure statement and Consumer Guide to Agency Relationships under Ohio Revised Code Chapter 4735, the Residential Property Disclosure Form, and the Real Estate Recovery Fund.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward Ohio's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
